name: Bundles/transaction
description: Bundle transaction conformance tests. POSTs a type=transaction Bundle to the FHIR base URL. The engine has no dedicated transaction operation code — type.code='transaction' is unrecognised by DeriveMethod and falls through to POST, and with no url/resource/params the request URL resolves to the empty relative path (the server base), which is exactly the transaction endpoint. The Bundle bodies are static fixtures (server-assigned IDs) so no client IDs are needed. Idempotency is exercised via request.ifNoneExist (conditional create). Also covers urn:uuid fullUrl reference resolution (Bundle-internal Patient<-Observation reference resolved to the server-assigned id), ported from BundleTransactionTests.GivenABundleWithSingleGeneratedId_WhenSubmittingATransaction_ThenTheSingleGeneratedIDIsResolvedProperly — a genuine gap not previously covered here.

variable.name: txUuidPatientId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Patient created via urn:uuid fullUrl in the tx-urnuuid-bundle transaction, used for teardown

variable.name: txUuidObservationId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Observation created in the same transaction, used for teardown

test.name: Transaction creates resources
test.description: POST a transaction Bundle of 3 Patient creates to the base URL. The server must return 200 with a transaction-response Bundle of 3 entries, each with a 2xx response.status.

operation.description: POST transaction Bundle (3 Patient creates) to the base URL

assert.description: Response Bundle must be of type transaction-response
Every entry must report a 2xx response status

test.name: Resources are retrievable after transaction
test.description: After the transaction commits, a search by one of the bundle identifiers must return at least one match, proving the resources were persisted.

assert.description: At least one BTX-1 Patient must be found

test.name: Conditional create (If-None-Exist) is idempotent
test.description: POST a transaction Bundle containing one Patient create guarded by ifNoneExist for an identifier that already exists (BTX-1). The transaction must succeed (200) and the entry must report status 200 (matched existing, not re-created 201).

operation.description: POST transaction Bundle with ifNoneExist for the already-existing BTX-1

assert.description: The single entry must report status 200 (existing resource matched, not created)

test.name: urn:uuid fullUrl references are resolved to the server-assigned id
test.description: POST a transaction Bundle where a Patient entry is identified by fullUrl 'urn:uuid:...' and an Observation entry in the same Bundle references that Patient via the same urn:uuid. The server must replace the urn:uuid placeholder with the real server-assigned reference (e.g. 'Patient/abc123') in the persisted/returned Observation — not leave it as the literal urn:uuid or an unresolved value.

operation.description: POST transaction Bundle with a urn:uuid Patient<-Observation reference

assert.description: Both entries must report a 2xx response status
The Observation's subject reference must be resolved to a real Patient/{id} reference, not left as the urn:uuid placeholder
The resolved reference must not still contain the urn:uuid literal

operation.description: Teardown: delete the urn:uuid-created Patient by its server-assigned id
Teardown: delete the urn:uuid-created Observation by its server-assigned id
